stories.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
stories.simple_summary: Midjourney, a company that makes AI art tools, is in a legal fight with Hollywood studios. They want the studios to show what AI tools they themselves are using.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to lawyers and investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
stories.simple_summary: Alibaba, a big Chinese company, told its workers they cannot use Claude Code (an AI programming tool) because the company thinks it's risky.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you work at Alibaba, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
stories.simple_summary: Mistral AI is a company that makes AI tools similar to ChatGPT, and they've gotten a lot of money to grow their business. They say they want to make powerful AI available to everyday people.
stories.why_it_matters: This could mean more AI tools become available as options, but this is still mostly important to tech companies and investors right now.

stories.title: For one small business, AI was key to a quick start and expansion
stories.simple_summary: A small business owner used AI tools to help them start and grow their business faster.
stories.why_it_matters: If you own a small business, this shows AI can help save time on tasks like customer emails, scheduling, or paperwork.

stories.title: OpenAI’s apparent failure to visit key site raises questions over UK investment
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, apparently didn't visit an important location in the UK related to a planned investment there, raising questions about whether the deal will happen.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in the UK tech industry — not relevant to your daily life.

stories.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
stories.simple_summary: Fanfiction writers (people who write stories based on existing movies, books, or shows) are arguing with each other about whether using AI to help write stories is okay. Some people want to find and stop writers who use AI, but the methods they're using might accidentally catch innocent people.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're not a fanfiction writer, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: Libertyville middle school teacher charged with using AI to make child sexual abuse material from pictures of students
stories.simple_summary: A school teacher was arrested for using AI to create illegal images of children.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI tools can be used for serious crimes — it's important that laws exist to catch and punish people who do this.

stories.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
stories.simple_summary: TechCrunch published a guide explaining the confusing words and phrases people use when talking about AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is helpful if you read AI news and get confused by the jargon, but you don't need to do anything.

stories.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock: Alphabet vs. Micron Technology (the Winner May Surprise You)
stories.simple_summary: The Motley Fool is comparing whether Alphabet or Micron Technology might be the better company to invest money in, based on AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only for people thinking about buying stocks — nothing for most business owners to act on.

stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: There are other web browsers available besides Chrome and Safari, some of which have different approaches to how they work.
stories.why_it_matters: Only relevant if you're unhappy with your current web browser and want to try something different.

stories.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic, a company that makes AI tools like Claude, is building a new AI workbench tool designed to help scientists do research faster by organizing information and creating pictures and charts.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you're a researcher or scientist, this doesn't affect you directly right now.

stories.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
stories.simple_summary: Midjourney showed a video of a special AI ultrasound scanner they're developing that they say could eventually give cheap medical scans without radiation. However, they haven't shown much proof that it actually works yet.
stories.why_it_matters: This is still an experimental project — don't expect to see this in hospitals or spas anytime soon.
